description_long: This is a medium-sized, hand-crafted terracotta plant pot with a slightly tapered cylindrical shape and a wide, open mouth. The exterior is adorned with a bold geometric pattern in earthy red, cream, and black, reminiscent of traditional or indigenous pottery styles. The surface shows a matte, slightly rough finish, with visible brush strokes and some irregularities that add to its artisanal character. The interior is unglazed and reveals the natural reddish-brown clay, while the exterior pattern wraps around the entire circumference. The pot appears sturdy and thick-walled, suitable for supporting soil and a plant. There are no visible handles or drainage holes on the bottom.
description: A medium terracotta plant pot with bold geometric patterns in red, cream, and black, featuring a matte, slightly rough finish and a wide open mouth.
description_view_0: Front view shows the most prominent section of the geometric pattern, with strong black lines and white rectangular motifs on a red background.
description_view_1: This view highlights a lighter section of the pattern, with more cream color and a continuation of the geometric motif.
description_view_2: The pattern appears more angular and abstract here, with a focus on intersecting lines and less white detailing.
description_view_3: This side displays a balanced mix of red and cream, with the geometric pattern wrapping around and fewer white rectangles visible.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used in rustic gardens or patios for decorative planting
attribute_decorative_pattern: geometric lines and rectangles in earthy tones
